content: IndexedDB storage integrity pitfalls: (1) Default storage is 'best-effort' — Chrome evicts the entire origin (all IndexedDB + Cache + localStorage together) under storage pressure, not just one table. There is no partial eviction or graceful trimming. (2) Safari ITP 7-day eviction applies to browser visits; installed PWA home screen apps are EXEMPT per WebKit blog. (3) Browser crashes can cause ~8% data loss on transactions that were not committed to disk before crash (confirmed by Playwright testing). (4) navigator.storage.persist() should be called early in app lifecycle; if granted, data is only cleared by explicit user action. Denied on first visit commonly; higher grant rate if app is installed PWA. (5) After eviction, the database simply no longer exists — db.open() creates a new empty DB, no error. App must detect empty state and show data-loss warning or import prompt rather than silently displaying an empty app.
